title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: ksh bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for ksh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: KornShell (ksh) is a Unix shell developed by AT&T Bell Laboratories, which is backward-compatible with the Bourne shell (sh) and includes many features of the C shell. The most recent version is KSH-93. KornShell complies with the POSIX.2 standard (IEEE Std 1003.2-1992).Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): sudo ksh executes ksh scripts using bash instead of ksh when checking the checksum (JIRA:RHEL-92629)
affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 10 x86_64

fixes.description: sudo ksh executes ksh scripts using bash instead of ksh when checking the checksum

full_text:
An update for ksh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

KornShell (ksh) is a Unix shell developed by AT&T Bell Laboratories, which is backward-compatible with the Bourne shell (sh) and includes many features of the C shell. The most recent version is KSH-93. KornShell complies with the POSIX.2 standard (IEEE Std 1003.2-1992).Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): sudo ksh executes ksh scripts using bash instead of ksh when checking the checksum (JIRA:RHEL-92629)
